package router

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for gin_route_paths_repeated_as_raw_strings: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Repeated route path literals that should be centralized or grouped more intentionally.
type CaseGinRoutePathsRepeatedAsRawStringsDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseGinRoutePathsRepeatedAsRawStringsModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseGinRoutePathsRepeatedAsRawStringsRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseGinRoutePathsRepeatedAsRawStringsModel
}

type CaseGinRoutePathsRepeatedAsRawStringsService struct {
    repo *CaseGinRoutePathsRepeatedAsRawStringsRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseGinRoutePathsRepeatedAsRawStringsAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveGinRoutePathsRepeatedAsRawStrings(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseGinRoutePathsRepeatedAsRawStringsDTO) error {
    r := gin.Default()
    r.GET("/api/v1/users", func(c *gin.Context) {
        c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"path": "/api/v1/users"})
    })
    r.POST("/api/v1/users", func(c *gin.Context) {
        c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"path": "/api/v1/users"})
    })
    r.PUT("/api/v1/users", func(c *gin.Context) {
        c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"path": "/api/v1/users"})
    })
    _ = r
    return nil
}
